name: Chocolate-Gilded Danish Sugar Cones
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Generously grease 4 cookie sheets.
3. Beat butter and sugar in a large bowl until light and fluffy. Blend in flour.
4. In a clean, dry bowl, beat egg whites until frothy. Fold into the butter mixture with vanilla.
5. Using a measuring teaspoon, place 4 mounds of dough, about 2 inches apart, on each prepared cookie sheet.
6. Spread mounds with the back of a spoon dipped in water to form circles approximately 3 inches in diameter.
7. Bake 1 sheet at a time for 5-6 minutes or until edges are just barely golden. Do not overbake; otherwise, cookies become crisp too quickly and are difficult to shape.
8. Remove from oven and loosen each cookie with a thin spatula.
9. Shape each into a cone; cones become firm as they cool. If needed, return to oven briefly to soften.
10. Melt chocolate in a small bowl over hot (not boiling) water, stirring until smooth.
11. When all cookies are baked and cooled, dip the flared ends into melted chocolate.
12. Let stand until chocolate is set. Optionally serve cones by standing them in a bowl; adding about 1 inch of sugar to the bottom may help hold them upright.
13. Makes 16 cookies
